name: Natural Park Sierra de las Nieves - Puerto de la Mujer – circular from El Burgo
name_original: Parque Natural Sierra de las Nieves - Puerto de la Mujer – circular desde El Burgo
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Burgo offers routes through the Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park, characterized by mountainous terrain and varied landscapes. The region features significant elevation changes, providing challenging climbs and rewarding descents. Cyclists can explore areas with dense woodlands and open vistas, including views towards the Guadalhorce Reservoir. The routes often traverse natural parkland, showcasing the area's geological features and natural beauty. Best touring cycling routes around El Burgo The most popular touring cycling route is Natural Park Sierra de las Nieves - Puerto de la Mujer – circular from El Burgo, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route offers a balanced touring cycling experience through the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sierra de las Nieves: Panoramic Route, a difficult 21.8 miles (35.1 km) path. This route provides extensive panoramic views across the Sierra de las Nieves, featuring significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Guadalhorce Reservoir – Guadalhorce Reservoir loop from Ardales, a 12.4 miles (19.9 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Guadalhorce Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Touring cycling around El Burgo is defined by mountainous terrain, natural parkland, and reservoir views. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate routes to more challenging difficult paths. The routes in El Burgo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 750 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Burgo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around El Burgo?
A: Touring cycling around El Burgo primarily features mountainous terrain within the Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including dense woodlands and open vistas, with significant elevation changes offering both challenging climbs and rewarding descents. Many routes also provide views towards the Guadalhorce Reservoir.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in El Burgo?
A: While El Burgo is known for its challenging mountainous terrain, there are options for less experienced cyclists. Out of 36 available routes, 5 are rated as moderate, and 1 is easy. A good moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974523946/natural-park-sierra-de-las-nieves-puerto-de-la-mujer-circular-from-el-burgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park Sierra de las Nieves - Puerto de la Mujer – circular from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.6 miles (20.3 km) long and takes about 2 hours.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the El Burgo area?
A: El Burgo is ideal for experienced touring cyclists, with 30 out of 36 routes rated as difficult. For a significant challenge and extensive panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974538837/sierra-de-las-nieves-panoramic-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Nieves: Panoramic Route&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 21.8 miles (35.1 km) path with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33819928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – Ardales Town Center loop from Ardales&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 70 km.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in El Burgo?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes around El Burgo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974523946/natural-park-sierra-de-las-nieves-puerto-de-la-mujer-circular-from-el-burgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park Sierra de las Nieves - Puerto de la Mujer – circular from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;. For a longer, more challenging loop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38190468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Viento – Wind Pass loop from Los Peñoncillos&lt;/a&gt; covers over 50 km.

Q: What can I see or explore along the touring cycling routes near El Burgo?
A: The routes often traverse natural parkland, offering geological features and natural beauty. You can encounter several mountain passes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wind Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Mujer&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Ventilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/590713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Grande of Yunquera&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also feature scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3332090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;road sections with views of the Sierra de las Nieves&lt;/a&gt; and the Guadalhorce Reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Burgo?
A: The mountainous terrain of El Burgo means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher altitudes within the Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in El Burgo typically take to complete?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in El Burgo varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974523946/natural-park-sierra-de-las-nieves-puerto-de-la-mujer-circular-from-el-burgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park Sierra de las Nieves - Puerto de la Mujer – circular from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33819928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – Ardales Town Center loop from Ardales&lt;/a&gt;, can require 5 hours or more due to their length and significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in El Burgo?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Burgo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountainous terrain, the stunning panoramic views across the Sierra de las Nieves, and the diverse landscapes that include both dense woodlands and open vistas.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974538837/sierra-de-las-nieves-panoramic-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Nieves: Panoramic Route&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer extensive views. Many routes also feature &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3332090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;road sections with views of the Sierra de las Nieves&lt;/a&gt;, and you can enjoy vistas of the Guadalhorce Reservoir, particularly on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadalhorce Reservoir – Guadalhorce Reservoir loop from Ardales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Burgo?
A: There are 36 touring cycling routes available around El Burgo on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy to very difficult, with a strong emphasis on challenging mountainous terrain.

Q: Is it possible to combine touring cycling with exploring nearby towns or attractions?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass through or near points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33819928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – Ardales Town Center loop from Ardales&lt;/a&gt; connects two towns, allowing for exploration. You can also find routes that lead to specific attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Ventilla&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of the Guadalhorce Reservoir, providing opportunities to combine your ride with sightseeing.
